lines.translation: This semester, besides Chinese class, Lisa also chose a Chinese history course. China is one of the world's ancient civilizations, with a written history of over four thousand years. To learn more about Chinese history, she asked Li Wen to take her to the National Museum of China. Zhang Tianming and Ke Lin are also interested in Chinese history. Hearing that Lisa was going to visit the museum, they wanted to go along too. Li Wen happily agreed.

The National Museum of China is very large, and Chinese history is only a part of it. Li Wen suggested they only look at a few dynasties today and visit again later to see more. Lisa and the others thought this was a great suggestion.
Since you are all students, let me first introduce you to the most important educator in Chinese history. Do you know who it is?
Is it Confucius?
Correct. Confucius is the greatest educator and thinker in Chinese history, and he still has a great influence on Chinese education today.
I have a T-shirt with a saying from Confucius on it: "Isn't it a pleasure to have friends coming from afar?"
Now we've arrived at the Qin dynasty exhibition hall. Qin Shi Huang was the first emperor of China.
My history teacher said that Qin Shi Huang made great contributions to the unification of China.
Right, he also unified the written script.
And he made the common people build the Great Wall.
Was everything Qin Shi Huang did right?
He killed several hundred scholars and burned many ancient books, right?
He also made many, many people build palaces and his tomb for him.
You are all correct. Look, these are the Terracotta Warriors of Qin Shi Huang. After he died, he still wanted so many "soldiers and horses" to protect him.

The Qin dynasty didn't last long. The next important dynasty was the Han dynasty. The Han dynasty made great progress in politics, economics, and many other areas. The name of the Han ethnic group comes from the Han dynasty.
The "Silk Road" is also related to the Han dynasty, right?
Correct. Trade with the West began in the Han dynasty.
Look, we've come to the Tang dynasty. My dad often tells me that Chinese Tang poetry is the most famous.
That's right, many great poets appeared in the Tang dynasty. Whose poems have you studied?
I've studied Li Bai's poems.
Li Bai is one of the most famous poets of the Tang dynasty. Also, because the Tang dynasty's economy and culture were very developed, many international students came to study.
Just like us.
Did you know that in history, China's science and technology were once very advanced?
Are you talking about the Four Great Inventions?
I know, they are papermaking, gunpowder, the compass, and movable-type printing.

Correct. Papermaking and gunpowder were invented relatively early. Movable-type printing was invented in the Song dynasty, and the compass also reached a high level of development during the Song dynasty.
We have now come to China's last dynasty: the Qing dynasty.
Why is it the last one?
Because in 1911, Sun Yat-sen led a revolution, and in 1912, the Republic of China was established. After that, there were no more emperors.
We're really something, we've walked through several thousand years in just a couple of hours.
I think I hear a sound.
What sound?
The footsteps of history.
I heard a sound too.
You mean the sound of a stomach growling...
Exactly.
What are you guys talking about?

example_sentence.translation: China is one of the world's ancient civilizations.

language_notes.label: a
language_notes.focus_text: 有朋自远方来,不亦乐乎
language_notes.explanation_en: This saying is from The Analects of Confucius. It is often quoted to mean, "Isn't it a great joy to have friends from far away?" According to the Tang commentator 孔颖达 (Kǒng Yǐngdá, 574–648), 朋 here means “classmate” or “study companion” (同门). The Song philosopher 朱熹 (Zhū Xī, 1130-1200) glosses 朋 as “kindred spirit” or “like-minded person” (lit. same type [of person]), 同类. [自 = 从; 不亦…乎 = 不是…吗; 乐 = 快乐].

language_notes.label: b
language_notes.explanation_en: 汉族 is short for 汉民族, the Han ethnic group.

language_notes.label: c
language_notes.explanation_en: 丝绸之路 is the Chinese translation of "Silk Road," a term coined by the German traveler and geographer Ferdinand von Richthofen (1833-1905).

language_notes.label: d
language_notes.explanation_en: 进行 must be followed by a two-syllable verb denoting an extended activity of some importance, e.g., 研究, 讨论, 教育, 贸易, etc. It can't be used with verbs denoting brief, ordinary activities. For instance, we wouldn't say 进行洗澡. 进行 can be omitted without affecting the meaning. 进行贸易 is more formal than 贸易.

language_notes.label: e
language_notes.explanation_en: 那时 is short for 那个时候 (at that time).

language_notes.label: f
language_notes.explanation_en: “Revolution” here refers to 辛亥革命 (Xīnhài Gémìng), the Xinhai Revolution, also known as the Revolution of 1911.

grammar_sections.title: 之一
grammar_sections.explanation_en: 之 is a structural particle inherited from classical Chinese. Among its many usages, it can be used in a way similar to 的. 之一 means “one of.”

examples.hanzi: 中国是世界文明古国之一。
examples.pinyin: Zhōngguó shì shìjiè wénmíng gǔguó zhī yī.
examples.translation: China is one of the world's ancient civilizations.

examples.hanzi: 造纸是中国的四大发明之一。
examples.pinyin: Zàozhǐ shì Zhōngguó de sì dà fāmíng zhī yī.
examples.translation: Papermaking is one of the four great inventions of China.

examples.hanzi: 石林是云南最有名的旅游景点之一。
examples.pinyin: Shílín shì Yúnnán zuì yǒumíng de lǚyóu jǐngdiǎn zhī yī.
examples.translation: The Stone Forest is one of the most famous tourist sights in Yunnan.

usage_notes: 之 must be preceded by a phrase denoting scope, such as 世界文明古国 (world's ancient civilizations), 四大发明 (four great inventions), and 旅游景点 (tourist sights).

grammar_sections.title: 其中
grammar_sections.explanation_en: 其 is a pronoun with the same meaning as 那 or 那些. 其中 means “among them” or “within it.”

examples.hanzi: 中国国家博物馆很大，中国历史只是其中的一部分。
examples.pinyin: Zhōngguó Guójiā Bówùguǎn hěn dà, Zhōngguó lìshǐ zhǐ shì qízhōng de yí bùfen.
examples.translation: The National Museum of China is very large, and the exhibit of Chinese history is only one part of it.

examples.hanzi: 我去过中国很多省旅游，其中我对云南和四川的印象最深。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ qùguo Zhōngguó hěn duō shěng lǚyóu, qízhōng wǒ duì Yúnnán hé Sìchuān de yìnxiàng zuì shēn.
examples.translation: Of all the Chinese provinces I've traveled to, Yunnan and Sichuan had the biggest impression on me.

examples.hanzi: 上个学期我选了三门课，其中金融课最难。
examples.pinyin: Shàng ge xuéqī wǒ xuǎn le sān mén kè, qízhōng jīnróng kè zuì nán.
examples.translation: Last semester I took three courses. Among them, Finance was the hardest.

usage_notes: When using 其中, there must be an antecedent to 其 to make it clear to what 其 refers. In (A), it is 中国国家博物馆 (the National Museum of China); in (B), it is 很多省 (many provinces); and in (C), it is 三门课 (three courses).

context_examples.translation: This semester, besides Chinese class, Lisa also chose a Chinese history course. global_config.visual_style: Cinematic and realistic, bright modern lighting. Focus on natural character interactions within a grand museum setting.
global_config.setting_summary_cn: 四位学生（丽莎、张天明、柯林、李文）相约参观中国国家博物馆，开启他们的中国历史文化之旅。
global_config.setting_summary_en: Four students (Lisa, Zhang Tianming, Ke Lin, Li Wen) meet up to visit the National Museum of China, beginning their tour of Chinese history and culture.

character_definitions.name: 丽莎
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A curious Caucasian female student in her early 20s, with long blonde hair, wearing a stylish but comfortable outfit for a museum visit.

character_definitions.name: 李文
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A friendly and knowledgeable Chinese female student in her early 20s, acting as the guide, with dark hair, dressed in a smart-casual style.

character_definitions.name: 张天明
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: An energetic Chinese-American male student in his early 20s, with a friendly and open expression, wearing casual clothes like a graphic T-shirt and jeans.

character_definitions.name: 柯林
character_definitions.appearance_description_en: A thoughtful Caucasian male student in his early 20s, with glasses, showing keen interest in the exhibits, dressed in a preppy style.

hanzi: 丽莎这个学期除了中文课以外还选了一门中国历史课。中国是世界文明古国之一，有文字记载的历史就有四千多年。为了对中国历史有更多的了解，她请李文带她去中国国家博物馆参观。张天明和柯林对中国历史也有兴趣，听说丽莎要参观博物馆，也要跟着一起去。李文很高兴地答应了。
中国国家博物馆很大，中国历史只是其中的一部分。李文建议今天只看几个朝代，以后再慢慢参观。丽莎他们觉得这个建议很好。
pinyin: Lìshā zhège xuéqī chúle Zhōngwén kè yǐwài hái xuǎn le yī mén Zhōngguó lìshǐ kè Zhōngguó shì shìjiè wénmíng gǔguó zhīyī yǒu wénzì jìzǎi de lìshǐ jiù yǒu sìqiān duō nián wèile duì Zhōngguó lìshǐ yǒu gèng duō de liǎojiě tā qǐng Lǐ Wén dài tā qù Zhōngguó guójiā bówùguǎn cānguān Zhāng Tiānmíng hé Kē Lín duì Zhōngguó lìshǐ yě yǒu xìngqù tīngshuō Lìshā yào cānguān bówùguǎn yě yào gēnzhe yìqǐ qù Lǐ Wén hěn gāoxìng de dāying le
Zhōngguó guójiā bówùguǎn hěn dà Zhōngguó lìshǐ zhǐshì qízhōng de yī bùfen Lǐ Wén jiànyì jīntiān zhǐ kàn jǐ gè cháodài yǐhòu zài mànmàn cānguān Lìshā tāmen juéde zhège jiànyì hěn hǎo
translation: This semester, besides Chinese class, Lisa also chose a Chinese history course. China is one of the world's ancient civilizations, with a written history of over four thousand years. To learn more about Chinese history, she asked Li Wen to take her to the National Museum of China. Zhang Tianming and Ke Lin are also interested in Chinese history. Hearing that Lisa was going to visit the museum, they wanted to go along too. Li Wen happily agreed.

The National Museum of China is very large, and Chinese history is only a part of it. Li Wen suggested they only look at a few dynasties today and visit again later to see more. Lisa and the others thought this was a great suggestion.

scenes.scene_goal: Introduce the characters, their motivation for visiting the museum, and their arrival.
scenes.scene_description_cn: 蒙太奇镜头：丽莎在大学校园里兴奋地和李文、张天明、柯林商量去博物馆。接着，四人一起抵达宏伟的国家博物馆门口，仰望建筑。进入大厅后，他们被其规模所震撼，李文向大家提议先看几个朝代，众人点头同意。
scenes.setting_en: A sunny university campus, followed by the grand exterior and vast main hall of the National Museum of China.
scenes.camera_movement: Quick cuts, followed by a slow pan up and a wide tracking shot.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Montage: Lisa, a blonde woman, excitedly talks with her three friends (Li Wen, Zhang Tianming, Ke Lin) on a university campus. Cut to the four of them arriving at the magnificent National Museum of China, looking up in awe. Wide shot as they enter the vast, brightly lit main hall. Li Wen gestures to the group, suggesting a plan, and they all nod in agreement. Cinematic, sense of wonder.

voice_over.speaker: 旁白
voice_over.hanzi: 丽莎这个学期除了中文课以外还选了一门中国历史课。为了对中国历史有更多的了解，她请李文带她去中国国家博物馆参观。张天明和柯林听说丽莎要参观博物馆，也要跟着一起去。李文很高兴地答应了。中国国家博物馆很大，李文建议今天只看几个朝代，丽莎他们觉得这个建议很好。
voice_over.pinyin: Lìshā zhège xuéqī chúle Zhōngwén kè yǐwài hái xuǎn le yī mén Zhōngguó lìshǐ kè. Wèile duì Zhōngguó lìshǐ yǒu gèng duō de liǎojiě, tā qǐng Lǐ Wén dài tā qù Zhōngguó guójiā bówùguǎn cānguān. Zhāng Tiānmíng hé Kē Lín tīngshuō Lìshā yào cānguān bówùguǎn, yě yào gēnzhe yìqǐ qù. Lǐ Wén hěn gāoxìng de dāying le. Zhōngguó guójiā bówùguǎn hěn dà, Lǐ Wén jiànyì jīntiān zhǐ kàn jǐ gè cháodài, Lìshā tāmen juéde zhège jiànyì hěn hǎo.
voice_over.translation: This semester, besides Chinese class, Lisa also chose a Chinese history course. To learn more about Chinese history, she asked Li Wen to take her to the National Museum of China. Hearing that Lisa was going to visit the museum, Zhang Tianming and Ke Lin wanted to go along too. Li Wen happily agreed. The National Museum of China is very large, so Li Wen suggested they only look at a few dynasties today. Lisa and the others thought this was a great suggestion.

hanzi: 你们都是学生，我就先给你们介绍中国历史上最重要的一位教育家。你们知道是谁吗？
pinyin: nǐmen dōu shì xuésheng wǒ jiù xiān gěi nǐmen jièshào Zhōngguó lìshǐ shàng zuì zhòngyào de yī wèi jiàoyùjiā nǐmen zhīdào shì shéi ma
translation: Since you are all students, let me first introduce you to the most important educator in Chinese history. Do you know who it is?

scenes.scene_goal: Li Wen begins the tour by leading the group to the first exhibit and posing a question.
scenes.scene_description_cn: 李文带领大家走到一尊庄严的孔子雕像前。她带着神秘的微笑转向朋友们，开始提问，引导他们进入历史话题。
scenes.setting_en: Inside the museum, in a quiet exhibit hall, in front of a large, detailed statue of Confucius.
scenes.camera_movement: The camera follows the group and then holds a steady shot on Li Wen.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Li Wen leads her three friends through a museum hall, stopping before a majestic statue of Confucius. She turns to them with a warm, engaging smile, gesturing towards the statue as she asks a question.

voice_over.speaker: 李文
voice_over.hanzi: 你们都是学生，我就先给你们介绍中国历史上最重要的一位教育家。你们知道是谁吗？
voice_over.pinyin: nǐmen dōu shì xuésheng wǒ jiù xiān gěi nǐmen jièshào Zhōngguó lìshǐ shàng zuì zhòngyào de yī wèi jiàoyùjiā nǐmen zhīdào shì shéi ma
voice_over.translation: Since you are all students, let me first introduce you to the most important educator in Chinese history. Do you know who it is?

hanzi: 是孔子吧？
pinyin: shì Kǒngzǐ ba
translation: Is it Confucius?

scenes.scene_goal: Lisa shows her knowledge by making an educated guess.
scenes.scene_description_cn: 镜头给到丽莎，她略作思考，眼神明亮，带着一点不确定但又充满期待地回答。
scenes.setting_en: In front of the Confucius statue in the museum.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Close-up on Lisa's face. She looks thoughtful for a moment, then her eyes light up as she offers a tentative answer, looking at Li Wen for confirmation.

hanzi: 对。孔子是中国历史上最伟大的教育家和思想家，到现在对中国教育还有很大的影响。
pinyin: duì kǒng zǐ shì zhōng guó lì shǐ shàng zuì wěi dà de jiào yù jiā hé sī xiǎng jiā dào xiàn zài duì zhōng guó jiào yù hái yǒu hěn dà de yǐng xiǎng
translation: Correct. Confucius is the greatest educator and thinker in Chinese history, and he still has a great influence on Chinese education today.

scenes.scene_goal: Li Wen confirms Lisa's answer and provides more context about Confucius's significance.
scenes.scene_description_cn: 李文赞许地点头，脸上露出欣慰的笑容。她一边解释，一边用目光示意大家看这尊充满智慧的雕像。
scenes.setting_en: In front of the Confucius statue in the museum.
scenes.camera_movement: Starts on Li Wen's face, then pans slightly to include the group's attentive expressions.
scenes.video_prompt_en: Medium shot of Li Wen smiling warmly and nodding at Lisa. She then addresses the whole group, explaining with reverence as she gestures towards the Confucius statue. The others listen intently.

voice_over.speaker: 李文
voice_over.hanzi: 对。孔子是中国历史上最伟大的教育家和思想家，到现在对中国教育还有很大的影响。
voice_over.pinyin: duì. kǒng zǐ shì zhōng guó lì shǐ shàng zuì wěi dà de jiào yù jiā hé sī xiǎng jiā, dào xiàn zài duì zhōng guó jiào yù hái yǒu hěn dà de yǐng xiǎng.
voice_over.translation: Correct. Confucius is the greatest educator and thinker in Chinese history, and he still has a great influence on Chinese education today.

hanzi: 你们知道吗，在历史上，中国科学技术曾经是很先进的。
你是说四大发明吗？
我知道，是造纸、火药、指南针、活字印刷。
对，造纸、火药发明得较早。活字印刷是宋朝发明的，指南针也在宋朝发展到了很高的水平。
pinyin: nǐmen zhīdào ma zài lìshǐ shàng zhōngguó kēxué jìshù céngjīng shì hěn xiānjìn de
nǐ shì shuō sì dà fāmíng ma
wǒ zhīdào shì zàozhǐ huǒyào zhǐnánzhēn huózì yìnshuā
duì zào zhǐ huǒ yào fā míng de jiào zǎo huó zì yìn shuā shì sòng cháo fā míng de zhǐ nán zhēn yě zài sòng cháo fā zhǎn dào le hěn gāo de shuǐ píng
translation: Did you know that in history, China's science and technology were once very advanced?
Are you talking about the Four Great Inventions?
I know, they are papermaking, gunpowder, the compass, and movable-type printing.

Correct. Papermaking and gunpowder were invented relatively early. Movable-type printing was invented in the Song dynasty, and the compass also reached a high level of development during the Song dynasty.

scenes.scene_goal: 通过讨论四大发明，展示中国古代科技的先进性。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 李文自豪地向朋友们介绍中国古代科技的辉煌。当她提到四大发明时，画面中快速闪现四大发明的动画演示，张天明兴奋地抢答，李文补充说明了发明的朝代。
scenes.setting_en: A well-lit section of a modern history museum, in front of a large interactive display about ancient Chinese science and technology.
scenes.camera_movement: Starts with a medium shot of the group. Zooms in on Li Wen as she speaks, then quick cuts to animated sequences for each invention. Pans across the group as they react.
scenes.video_prompt_en: A group of four diverse university students in a modern museum. Li Wen, the Chinese guide, speaks enthusiastically. As Zhang Tianming lists ancient inventions, hyper-stylized, fast-paced 3D animations of papermaking, black powder exploding in slow motion, an ornate ancient compass spinning, and Chinese movable type blocks assembling appear as overlays. The other students look impressed and engaged.

voice_over.speaker: 李文
voice_over.hanzi: 你们知道吗，在历史上，中国科学技术曾经是很先进的。
voice_over.pinyin: nǐmen zhīdào ma zài lìshǐ shàng zhōngguó kēxué jìshù céngjīng shì hěn xiānjìn de
voice_over.translation: Did you know that in history, China's science and technology were once very advanced?

hanzi: 我们现在来到中国最后一个朝代：清朝。
为什么是最后一个？
因为一九一一年孙中山领导革命，一九一二年建立了中华民国，就再也没有皇帝了。
pinyin: wǒ men xiàn zài lái dào zhōng guó zuì hòu yí gè cháo dài qīng cháo
wèi shén me shì zuì hòu yí gè
yīnwèi yī jiǔ yī yī nián Sūn Zhōngshān lǐngdǎo gémìng yī jiǔ yī èr nián jiànlì le Zhōnghuá Mínguó jiù zài yě méiyǒu huángdì le
translation: We have now come to China's last dynasty: the Qing dynasty.
Why is it the last one?
Because in 1911, Sun Yat-sen led a revolution, and in 1912, the Republic of China was established. After that, there were no more emperors.

scenes.scene_goal: 解释清朝是中国最后一个朝代的原因，引出中华民国的建立。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 场景转换到清朝展厅，李文介绍这是最后一个朝代。丽莎好奇地提问，李文随即解释了孙中山领导的革命。画面中穿插泛黄的历史影像资料，表现时代的更迭。
scenes.setting_en: A different museum hall, decorated in the style of the Qing Dynasty, with large portraits of emperors and artifacts in glass cases.
scenes.camera_movement: Tracking shot as the group walks into the new hall. Close-up on Lisa's curious face. Cut to a montage of sepia-toned historical photos/drawings of the 1911 revolution and Sun Yat-sen.
scenes.shot_type: Tracking shot, Medium shot, Close-up, Archival footage insert
scenes.video_prompt_en: The four friends walk into a grand museum hall themed after the Qing Dynasty, filled with imperial artifacts. Li Wen gestures around. Close up on Lisa asking a question. Quick montage of vintage, sepia-toned photos and film clips showing Sun Yat-sen, revolutionary soldiers, and the end of the imperial era. The friends look thoughtful.

voice_over.speaker: 李文
voice_over.hanzi: 我们现在来到中国最后一个朝代：清朝。
voice_over.pinyin: wǒ men xiàn zài lái dào zhōng guó zuì hòu yí gè cháo dài qīng cháo
voice_over.translation: We have now come to China's last dynasty: the Qing dynasty.

hanzi: 我们真不简单，一两个小时就走过了几千年。
我好像听到一个声音。
什么声音？
历史的脚步声。
我也听到了一个声音。
你要说肚子叫的……
对了。
你们在说什么呀？
pinyin: wǒmen zhēn bù jiǎndān yī liǎng ge xiǎoshí jiù zǒuguò le jǐ qiān nián
wǒ hǎoxiàng tīngdào yí ge shēngyīn
shén me shēng yīn
lì shǐ de jiǎo bù shēng
wǒ yě tīng dào le yí gè shēng yīn
nǐ yào shuō dùzi jiào de
duìle
nǐmen zài shuō shénme ya
translation: We're really something, we've walked through several thousand years in just a couple of hours.
I think I hear a sound.
What sound?
The footsteps of history.
I heard a sound too.
You mean the sound of a stomach growling...
Exactly.
What are you guys talking about?

scenes.scene_goal: 以一个轻松幽默的结尾，总结这次历史之旅，同时展现朋友间的有趣互动。
scenes.scene_description_cn: 参观接近尾声，大家走在博物馆宽敞的走廊上。张天明感慨万千。柯林突然说听到声音，李文诗意地解释为‘历史的脚步声’。但张天明也听到了声音，丽莎一语道破是他肚子饿了，引得大家发笑，只有李文还有点没反应过来，气氛轻松愉快。
scenes.setting_en: The main, spacious, modern exit hall of the museum, with high ceilings and large windows showing the late afternoon sun.
scenes.camera_movement: Wide shot of the group walking, then a slow zoom on Zhang Tianming as he speaks. Quick cuts between Ke Lin, Lisa, and Li Wen during their exchange. Close-up on Zhang Tianming's face, then a cut to Lisa teasing him. Ends with a medium shot of the group laughing.
scenes.video_prompt_en: As the four friends walk through the museum's bright, modern grand hall, Zhang Tianming looks reflective. Ke Lin looks around quizzically. A faint, echoing footstep sound is heard. Li Wen smiles poetically. Then a loud stomach growl sound is heard. Close up on Zhang Tianming looking embarrassed. Lisa smirks and teases him. The group bursts into laughter, except for Li Wen who looks slightly confused. Golden hour light streams through the windows.

voice_over.speaker: 张天明
voice_over.hanzi: 我们真不简单，一两个小时就走过了几千年。
voice_over.pinyin: wǒmen zhēn bù jiǎndān yī liǎng ge xiǎoshí jiù zǒuguò le jǐ qiān nián
voice_over.translation: We're really something, we've walked through several thousand years in just a couple of hours.

global_config.teaching_style: Clear, structured, and encouraging, focusing on breaking down sentences and vocabulary for easy understanding.
global_config.visual_style: Clean and modern, suitable for educational cards and animated text.

segments.segment_title: Setting the Scene: Lisa's History Course
segments.teaching_goal: To understand the sentence structure '除了...以外还...' (besides... also...) and learn vocabulary related to school courses.

narration.subtitle_en: This first part of the sentence sets the scene. It says that this semester, besides her Chinese class, Lisa also chose a Chinese history course. The key structure here is [zh:除了...以外，还...], which means 'besides... also...'. We see she [zh:选了], or 'chose', a course. The word for course is [zh:课], and the measure word for courses is [zh:门].

on_screen_text.focus_text: 丽莎这个学期除了中文课以外还选了一门中国历史课。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Lìshā zhège xuéqī chúle Zhōngwén kè yǐwài hái xuǎn le yī mén Zhōngguó lìshǐ kè.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: This semester, besides Chinese class, Lisa also chose a Chinese history course.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A school term or semester.
To select or choose, often used for picking courses.
The study of past events.

segments.visual_notes: Animate the structure '除了...以外还...' to show the relationship between the two courses.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to describe China as an ancient civilization and understand the phrase '...之一' (one of...).

narration.subtitle_en: Next, we get some background on China. It's described as one of the world's ancient civilizations. The phrase is [zh:世界文明古国之一]. Let's break it down: [zh:世界] is 'world', [zh:文明] is 'civilization', and [zh:古国] is 'ancient country'. The key grammar point here is [zh:之一], meaning 'one of'. The sentence continues that China has a written history of over four thousand years. [zh:有文字记载的历史] means 'a history with written records'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 中国是世界文明古国之一，有文字记载的历史就有四千多年。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhōngguó shì shìjiè wénmíng gǔguó zhī yī, yǒu wénzì jìzǎi de lìshǐ jiù yǒu sìqiān duō nián.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: China is one of the world's ancient civilizations, with a written history of over four thousand years.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Refers to a society, its culture, and its way of life during a particular period of time.
A structure used to indicate that something is one member of a larger group.
Characters or the written form of a language.
To put down in writing; an account or record.

segments.visual_notes: Visually break down the phrase '世界文明古国之一' into its components.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn phrases for expressing purpose ('为了') and arranging activities ('请', '带', '参观').

narration.subtitle_en: This part explains Lisa's motivation. It starts with [zh:为了], which means 'in order to'. Her goal is to have a better understanding of Chinese history, or [zh:对中国历史有更多的了解]. To achieve this, she asks Li Wen to take her to the National Museum of China. The verb [zh:请] means 'to ask' or 'to invite'. And the purpose of the visit is to [zh:参观], which means 'to visit' a place like a museum or exhibition.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 为了对中国历史有更多的了解，她请李文带她去中国国家博物馆参观。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wèile duì Zhōngguó lìshǐ yǒu gèng duō de liǎojiě, tā qǐng Lǐ Wén dài tā qù Zhōngguó Guójiā Bówùguǎn cānguān.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: To learn more about Chinese history, she asked Li Wen to take her to the National Museum of China.

highlight_words.explanation_en: To know or comprehend something.
A building in which objects of historical, scientific, artistic, or cultural interest are stored and exhibited.
To visit and look around a place, such as a museum, factory, or school.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon for 'purpose' (like a target) for '为了', then an icon for 'museum' for '参观'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express interest ('对...有兴趣') and the idea of joining or following ('跟着').

narration.subtitle_en: It turns out Lisa's friends are also interested. The phrase [zh:对...有兴趣] means 'to be interested in something'. We then see [zh:听说], which means 'to hear that' or 'it is said that'. Because they heard Lisa was going, they also wanted to [zh:跟着一起去], which means 'to go along with' her. [zh:跟着] means 'to follow'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 张天明和柯林对中国历史也有兴趣，听说丽莎要参观博物馆，也要跟着一起去。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhāng Tiānmíng hé Kē Lín duì Zhōngguó lìshǐ yě yǒu xìngqù, tīngshuō Lìshā yào cānguān bówùguǎn, yě yào gēnzhe yìqǐ qù.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Zhang Tianming and Ke Lin are also interested in Chinese history. Hearing that Lisa was going to visit the museum, they wanted to go along too.

highlight_words.explanation_en: The feeling of wanting to know or learn about something or someone.
Used to introduce information that one has heard from others.
To go or come after a person or thing proceeding in front.

segments.visual_notes: Use icons to show Zhang Tianming and Ke Lin following Lisa.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the verb '答应' (to agree) and understand the use of the adverbial particle '地'.

narration.subtitle_en: This is Li Wen's response. He happily agreed. The key verb here is [zh:答应], which means 'to agree' or 'to promise'. Notice the particle [zh:地] after [zh:很高兴]. It's used to turn an adjective, 'very happy', into an adverb, 'happily', describing how he agreed. This is a common grammatical pattern in written Chinese.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 李文很高兴地答应了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Lǐ Wén hěn gāoxìng de dāying le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Li Wen happily agreed.

highlight_words.explanation_en: To say yes to a request or to promise to do something.

segments.visual_notes: Highlight the 'Adjective + 地 + Verb' structure on screen.

segments.segment_title: At the Museum: Size and Scope
segments.teaching_goal: To understand the word '其中' (among which/them) and how it refers to a previously mentioned whole.

narration.subtitle_en: Now they are at the museum. The sentence first states that the National Museum of China is very large. Then it says that Chinese history is only one part of it. The key word here is [zh:其中], which means 'among them' or 'within it'. It refers back to the museum. [zh:一部分] means 'one part'. So, the history exhibit is just one part of the whole museum.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 中国国家博物馆很大，中国历史只是其中的一部分。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Zhōngguó Guójiā Bówùguǎn hěn dà, Zhōngguó lìshǐ zhǐ shì qízhōng de yī bùfen.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: The National Museum of China is very large, and Chinese history is only a part of it.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A pronoun that means 'among them' or 'within it', referring to a group or whole mentioned before.
A piece or a section of a whole.

segments.visual_notes: Show a large circle representing the museum, then highlight a smaller section within it labeled '中国历史'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary for making suggestions ('建议') and talking about historical periods ('朝代').

narration.subtitle_en: Because the museum is so big, Li Wen makes a suggestion. The verb for 'to suggest' is [zh:建议]. He suggests they only look at a few [zh:朝代], or 'dynasties', today. Then, [zh:以后], meaning 'in the future', they can come back and [zh:再慢慢参观], 'visit again slowly'. The word [zh:再] indicates doing something again in the future.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 李文建议今天只看几个朝代，以后再慢慢参观。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Lǐ Wén jiànyì jīntiān zhǐ kàn jǐ ge cháodài, yǐhòu zài mànmàn cānguān.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Li Wen suggested they only look at a few dynasties today and visit again later to see more.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An idea or plan put forward for consideration.
A line of hereditary rulers of a country.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a timeline with a few dynasties highlighted for 'today', and the rest grayed out for '以后'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to express agreement with an opinion using '觉得'.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisa and the others agree with the plan. The verb [zh:觉得] means 'to think' or 'to feel'. They use it here to express their opinion that Li Wen's suggestion, [zh:这个建议], is very good. This is a common and useful way to show you agree with someone's idea or share your own opinion.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 丽莎他们觉得这个建议很好。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Lìshā tāmen juéde zhège jiànyì hěn hǎo.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Lisa and the others thought this was a great suggestion.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used to express an opinion or feeling.

segments.visual_notes: Show a speech bubble with a thumbs-up icon next to Lisa's group.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary for introducing people and concepts, including '介绍', '重要', '教育家', and the polite measure word '位'.

narration.subtitle_en: Li Wen begins the tour. He starts by saying, 'Since you are all students...'. He then says he will first introduce the most important educator in Chinese history. The verb [zh:介绍] means 'to introduce'. He calls this person the [zh:最重要], or 'most important', [zh:教育家], 'educator'. Notice the measure word [zh:位], which is a polite measure word for people.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你们都是学生，我就先给你们介绍中国历史上最重要的一位教育家。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐmen dōu shì xuésheng, wǒ jiù xiān gěi nǐmen jièshào Zhōngguó lìshǐ shàng zuì zhòngyào de yī wèi jiàoyùjiā.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Since you are all students, let me first introduce you to the most important educator in Chinese history.

highlight_words.explanation_en: To present someone or something to others for the first time.
Of great significance or value.
A person who is an expert in the theories and methods of education.

segments.visual_notes: Highlight the word '位' and add a note: 'polite measure word for people'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the common question pattern '知道是...吗？' (Do you know who/what... is?).

narration.subtitle_en: Li Wen ends with a question to engage the students. He asks, 'Do you know who it is?'. The structure [zh:知道是谁吗] is a very common way to ask if someone knows the identity of a person. It's a simple and direct question that invites a response.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你们知道是谁吗？
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐmen zhīdào shì shéi ma?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Do you know who it is?

highlight_words.explanation_en: To have knowledge or information about something.

segments.visual_notes: A large question mark appears on screen.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how the particle '吧' is used to indicate a guess or suggestion.

narration.subtitle_en: One of the students makes a guess. 'Is it Confucius?'. The name for Confucius in Chinese is [zh:孔子]. The particle [zh:吧] at the end of the sentence is very important here. It turns a statement into a suggestion or a guess, indicating uncertainty. It's like saying 'It's Confucius, right?' or 'I guess it's Confucius?'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 是孔子吧？
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Shì Kǒngzǐ ba?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Is it Confucius?

highlight_words.explanation_en: A famous Chinese philosopher, teacher, and political figure (551–479 BC).
A sentence-final particle used to make a suggestion, soften a command, or indicate a guess.

segments.visual_notes: Highlight the character '吧' and show a thought bubble with a question mark inside.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the words '伟大' (great) and '思想家' (thinker) to describe historical figures.

narration.subtitle_en: Li Wen confirms the guess is correct by saying [zh:对], which means 'correct'. He then describes Confucius as the greatest educator and thinker in Chinese history. We see the word [zh:伟大], meaning 'great' or 'outstanding'. He is not only an [zh:教育家], 'educator', but also a [zh:思想家], 'thinker'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 对。孔子是中国历史上最伟大的教育家和思想家。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Duì. Kǒngzǐ shì Zhōngguó lìshǐ shàng zuì wěidà de jiàoyùjiā hé sīxiǎngjiā.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Correct. Confucius is the greatest educator and thinker in Chinese history.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used to describe someone or something as magnificent and highly important.
A person who thinks deeply and originally, especially a philosopher.

segments.visual_notes: Show a portrait of Confucius with the titles '教育家' and '思想家' next to it.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the composition and meaning of '伟大' and '思想家'.

narration.subtitle_en: Let's focus on these two descriptive words. First, [zh:伟大]. This means 'great' or 'magnificent'. It combines [zh:伟], meaning 'big' or 'robust', and [zh:大], which also means 'big'. The combination emphasizes immense scale and importance. Next is [zh:思想家]. This means 'thinker'. It's composed of [zh:思想], 'thought' or 'ideology', and [zh:家], a suffix for a specialist or expert in a field, like an artist or scientist.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 伟大 / 思想家
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: wěidà / sīxiǎngjiā
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: great, outstanding / thinker
on_screen_text.notes: 家 (jiā) is a suffix for an expert in a field.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used to describe someone or something as magnificent and highly important.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: 伟 (wěi) means 'big' or 'robust' and 大 (dà) also means 'big', together emphasizing immense importance.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A person who thinks deeply and originally, especially a philosopher.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: 思想 (sīxiǎng) means 'thought' and 家 (jiā) is a suffix for an expert in a field.

segments.visual_notes: Show the character breakdown for each word, e.g., 思想 + 家 → 思想家.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the phrase '对...有影响' (to have an influence on...) and related vocabulary.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence explains Confucius's lasting legacy. It says that 'even now', or [zh:到现在], he still has a great influence on Chinese education. The key phrase is [zh:对...有很大的影响], which means 'to have a great influence on something'. Here, the 'something' is [zh:中国教育], Chinese education.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 到现在对中国教育还有很大的影响。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: dào xiànzài duì Zhōngguó jiàoyù háiyǒu hěn dà de yǐngxiǎng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: and he still has a great influence on Chinese education today.

highlight_words.explanation_en: The process of receiving or giving systematic instruction.
The capacity to have an effect on the character, development, or behavior of someone or something.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a ripple effect emanating from Confucius towards a school icon representing 'education'.

segments.teaching_goal: To recognize and understand the famous saying '有朋自远方来，不亦乐乎'.

narration.subtitle_en: Here, a student shares a personal connection. He says his T-shirt has a saying from Confucius on it. The phrase [zh:一句话] means 'one sentence' or 'a saying'. The quote is a famous one: [zh:有朋自远方来，不亦乐乎]. This is from classical Chinese and means 'Isn't it a pleasure to have friends coming from afar?'. It's a very well-known expression of hospitality and friendship in China.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我的一件T恤衫上有孔子的一句话：“有朋自远方来，不亦乐乎。”
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ de yī jiàn T-xùshān shàng yǒu Kǒngzǐ de yī jù huà: “Yǒu péng zì yuǎnfāng lái, bù yì lè hū.”
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I have a T-shirt with a saying from Confucius on it: "Isn't it a pleasure to have friends coming from afar?"

highlight_words.explanation_en: A common piece of clothing.

highlight_words.word: 有朋自远方来，不亦乐乎
highlight_words.pinyin: Yǒu péng zì yuǎnfāng lái, bù yì lè hū
highlight_words.translation: Isn't it a pleasure to have friends coming from afar?
highlight_words.explanation_en: A famous quote from The Analects of Confucius expressing joy in welcoming friends from a distance.

segments.visual_notes: Display the quote in a stylized, calligraphic font.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary related to museum sections, including '秦朝' (Qin Dynasty) and '展厅' (exhibition hall).

narration.subtitle_en: The group moves to a new section. Li Wen announces, 'Now we have arrived at the Qin Dynasty exhibition hall.' The verb [zh:来到] means 'to arrive at' a place. The new location is the [zh:秦朝的展厅]. [zh:秦朝] is the 'Qin Dynasty', and [zh:展厅] is an 'exhibition hall'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 现在我们来到了秦朝的展厅。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Xiànzài wǒmen láidào le Qíncháo de zhǎntīng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Now we've arrived at the Qin dynasty exhibition hall.

highlight_words.explanation_en: The first dynasty of Imperial China, lasting from 221 to 206 BC.
A room or hall for displaying exhibits.

segments.visual_notes: Show a museum map with an arrow pointing to the '秦朝展厅'.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the titles '秦始皇' (Qin Shi Huang) and '皇帝' (emperor).

narration.subtitle_en: Li Wen introduces the key figure of this dynasty. He says, 'Qin Shi Huang was the first emperor of China.' The name [zh:秦始皇] literally means 'First Emperor of Qin'. The word for 'emperor' is [zh:皇帝]. This is a major fact in Chinese history.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 秦始皇是中国的第一个皇帝。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Qín Shǐhuáng shì Zhōngguó de dì yī gè huángdì.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Qin Shi Huang was the first emperor of China.

highlight_words.translation: Qin Shi Huang (First Emperor of Qin)
highlight_words.explanation_en: The founder of the Qin dynasty and the first emperor of a unified China.
The sovereign ruler of an empire.

segments.visual_notes: Show an illustration of Qin Shi Huang.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the phrase '对...有贡献' (to make a contribution to...) and the words '统一' and '贡献'.

narration.subtitle_en: Lisa adds what she learned in her history class. She says her teacher mentioned that Qin Shi Huang made great contributions to the unification of China. The key phrase is [zh:对...有很大的贡献], 'to have a great contribution to...'. The two important new words are [zh:统一], 'to unify', and [zh:贡献], 'contribution'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我的历史老师说秦始皇对统一中国有很大的贡献。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ de lìshǐ lǎoshī shuō Qínshǐhuáng duì tǒngyī Zhōngguó yǒu hěn dà de gòngxiàn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: My history teacher said that Qin Shi Huang made great contributions to the unification of China.

highlight_words.explanation_en: To bring together or combine as a whole.
A part played by a person or thing in bringing about a result or helping something to advance.

segments.visual_notes: Show a map of separate states merging into one unified China.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand the historical achievement of unifying the written script ('统一了文字').

narration.subtitle_en: Li Wen confirms and adds more information. He says, 'Right, he also unified the written script.' This reinforces the verb [zh:统一], 'to unify'. The object this time is [zh:文字], which means 'characters' or 'the written form of a language'. This was one of Qin Shi Huang's most significant achievements.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 对，他还统一了文字。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Duì, tā hái tǒngyī le wénzì.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Right, he also unified the written script.

highlight_words.explanation_en: The written form of a language.

segments.visual_notes: Show different ancient scripts morphing into a single, standardized script.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn vocabulary related to the Great Wall: '让', '老百姓', '修', and '长城'.

narration.subtitle_en: Another student chimes in with another famous fact. 'And he made the common people build the Great Wall.' The verb [zh:让] here means 'to make' or 'to order someone to do something'. The [zh:老百姓] are the 'common people'. Their task was to [zh:修长城], 'build the Great Wall'. [zh:修] can mean to repair or, in this context, to build.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 还让老百姓修长城。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Hái ràng lǎobǎixìng xiū Chángchéng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: And he made the common people build the Great Wall.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Ordinary people, as distinct from the aristocracy or ruling class.
Can mean to repair, but in the context of large construction projects, it means to build.
The Great Wall of China.

segments.visual_notes: Show an animation of people working on a section of the Great Wall.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn how to ask if someone's actions were right or wrong.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence asks a critical question about Emperor Qin Shi Huang. It breaks down into: 'the things Qin Shi Huang did' [zh:秦始皇做的事], 'all' [zh:都], and 'correct?' [zh:对吗]. The phrase [zh:做的事] literally means 'the things that were done'. The word [zh:都] emphasizes that the question applies to everything he did, not just some things. Finally, adding [zh:吗] at the end turns the statement 'they are all correct' into a yes-no question.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 秦始皇做的事都对吗？
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Qín Shǐhuáng zuò de shì dōu duì ma?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Was everything Qin Shi Huang did right?

highlight_words.explanation_en: A common structure where a verb phrase (做事) is modified by 的 to function as a noun phrase, meaning 'the things that one does/did'.
An adverb that indicates inclusiveness, placed before the verb to mean that the action applies to everyone or everything mentioned.

segments.visual_notes: Animate the sentence parts as they are explained. Show an icon of a question mark next to a portrait of Qin Shi Huang.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn vocabulary for negative historical events and how to use the tag question '是吗？'.

narration.subtitle_en: This line provides specific examples of Qin Shi Huang's controversial actions. It lists two things: first, [zh:他杀了几百个读书人], 'he killed several hundred scholars,' and second, [zh:烧了很多古书], 'burned many ancient books.' The phrase [zh:是吗] at the end acts as a tag question, similar to 'right?' or 'isn't that so?' in English. It's used to seek confirmation for the statements just made.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 他杀了几百个读书人，烧了很多古书，是吗？
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Tā shāle jǐ bǎi gè dúshūrén, shāole hěn duō gǔshū, shì ma?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: He killed several hundred scholars and burned many ancient books, right?

highlight_words.explanation_en: A verb indicating the action of killing.
Literally 'book-reading person,' this term refers to scholars or intellectuals in old China.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: Composed of 读 (to read), 书 (book), and 人 (person).

highlight_words.explanation_en: A verb for the action of burning something.
A compound word for ancient or classical books.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: Composed of 古 (ancient) and 书 (book).

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon of a person reading a book, then an icon of fire, to illustrate the two actions.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn to use the causative verb '让' to mean 'to make' or 'to have someone do something'.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence adds another of the emperor's actions. The key verb here is [zh:让], which means 'to let' or 'to make someone do something'. The structure is 'Subject + 让 + Person + Verb'. Here, he made [zh:很多很多人], 'many, many people,' build things for him. The repetition of 'many people' adds emphasis. The two things they built were [zh:宫殿], palaces, and [zh:坟墓], his tomb.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 他还让很多很多人给他修宫殿、修坟墓。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Tā hái ràng hěn duō hěn duō rén gěi tā xiū gōngdiàn, xiū fénmù.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: He also made many, many people build palaces and his tomb for him.

highlight_words.translation: to let, to allow, to make
highlight_words.explanation_en: A causative verb used to indicate that someone causes someone else to perform an action.
A verb that can mean to build, construct, or repair.
The residence of an emperor or king.
A place where a person, especially a person of importance, is buried.

segments.visual_notes: Animate the structure '让 + [person icon] + [build icon]' to show the causative meaning.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn a common phrase to agree with what others have said.

narration.subtitle_en: The guide starts by confirming the students' points. The phrase [zh:你们说得都很对] means 'You are all correct' or 'What you've all said is correct.' Let's break it down: [zh:你们说的] is 'what you said,' and [zh:都很对] means 'is all correct.' This is a very useful phrase for agreeing with multiple people or multiple points someone has made.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你们说得都很对。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐmen shuō de dōu hěn duì.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: You are all correct.

segments.visual_notes: Show a checkmark icon next to the text to emphasize agreement.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn the name for the Terracotta Warriors and how to introduce a famous sight.

narration.subtitle_en: Next, the guide directs everyone's attention with [zh:你们看], which means 'Look.' Then, she introduces one of China's most famous historical sites: [zh:秦始皇的兵马俑], the Terracotta Warriors of Qin Shi Huang. The name [zh:兵马俑] is very descriptive. It's made of three characters: [zh:兵] for soldiers, [zh:马] for horses, and [zh:俑] for burial figurines. So, it literally means 'soldier and horse figurines'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你们看，这就是秦始皇的兵马俑，
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐmen kàn, zhè jiùshì Qín Shǐhuáng de bīngmǎyǒng,
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Look, these are the Terracotta Warriors of Qin Shi Huang,

highlight_words.explanation_en: The collection of terracotta sculptures depicting the armies of Qin Shi Huang, the first Emperor of China.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: Composed of 兵 (bīng, soldier), 马 (mǎ, horse), and 俑 (yǒng, burial figurine).

segments.visual_notes: Show a picture of the Terracotta Warriors. Highlight the characters 兵, 马, and 俑 as they are explained.

segments.teaching_goal: Understand how to express sequence with '以后' and the concept of protection.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence explains the purpose of the Terracotta Army. It starts with a time phrase, [zh:他死了以后], meaning 'after he died.' The word [zh:还] means 'still,' showing his desire continued even after death. He still wanted 'so many soldiers and horses,' [zh:这么多兵马], to do what? To [zh:保护他], protect him. The quotation marks around '兵马' hint that they are not real soldiers and horses, but statues.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 他死了以后还让这么多“兵马”保护他。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: tā sǐle yǐhòu hái ràng zhème duō “bīngmǎ” bǎohù tā.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: After he died, he still wanted so many "soldiers and horses" to protect him.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used after a verb or a time phrase to mean 'after...'.
A verb meaning to guard or defend from harm.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: 保 (bǎo) means to protect or keep, and 护 (hù) also means to protect or guard. Together, they form a strong word for protection.

segments.visual_notes: Show an animation of the terracotta soldiers standing guard around a tomb.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn to describe the transition between dynasties.

narration.subtitle_en: Here, we move from one dynasty to the next. The first part, [zh:秦朝时间不长], states that 'The Qin dynasty's time was not long.' Then, the guide introduces the next major period: [zh:下一个重要的朝代是汉朝], 'The next important dynasty is the Han dynasty.' This is a simple and effective way to describe historical succession.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 秦朝时间不长，下一个重要的朝代是汉朝。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Qíncháo shíjiān bù cháng, xià yī gè zhòngyào de cháodài shì Hàncháo.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: The Qin dynasty didn't last long. The next important dynasty was the Han dynasty.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A line of hereditary rulers of a country.
An influential dynasty in Chinese history that lasted from 206 BC to 220 AD.

segments.visual_notes: Show a timeline with 'Qin' fading out and 'Han' appearing next to it.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn vocabulary to describe progress in politics and economics.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence highlights the accomplishments of the Han dynasty. It made great progress, [zh:有很大的发展], in several key areas. These include [zh:政治], politics, and [zh:经济], economics. The phrase [zh:各个方面] means 'in various aspects' or 'in every field,' showing that the progress was widespread. This is a great pattern for describing achievements.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 汉朝在政治、经济各个方面都有很大的发展。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Hàncháo zài zhèngzhì, jīngjì gègè fāngmiàn dōu yǒu hěn dà de fāzhǎn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: The Han dynasty made great progress in politics, economics, and many other areas.

highlight_words.explanation_en: The activities associated with the governance of a country or area.
The system of trade and industry by which the wealth of a country is made and used.
The process of developing or being developed.

segments.visual_notes: Use icons for 'politics' (e.g., a government building) and 'economics' (e.g., coins) to illustrate the fields of development.

segments.teaching_goal: Understand the origin of the name for the Han ethnic group.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence explains an important cultural connection. The name of China's largest ethnic group, the [zh:汉族] or Han people, comes directly from the [zh:汉朝], the Han dynasty. The structure [zh:...是从...来的] is used to explain origins. It literally means '...is from...came.' This shows the profound and lasting influence of the Han dynasty on Chinese identity.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 汉族这个名字就是从汉朝来的。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Hànzú zhège míngzi jiùshì cóng Hàncháo lái de.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: The name of the Han ethnic group comes from the Han dynasty.
on_screen_text.notes: The Han ethnic group (汉族) is the majority ethnic group in China.

highlight_words.explanation_en: The largest ethnic group in China and worldwide. The name is derived from the Han dynasty.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: Composed of 汉 (Hàn, from the Han dynasty) and 族 (zú, ethnic group/clan).

segments.visual_notes: Show the word '汉朝' transforming into '汉族' with an arrow to show the origin.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn to use '跟...有关系' to ask if two things are related.

narration.subtitle_en: A student asks a question here, connecting another famous concept to the Han dynasty. The key phrase is [zh:跟...有关系], which means 'is related to...' or 'has a connection with...'. The student is asking if the [zh:丝绸之路], the Silk Road, is also related to the Han dynasty. The particle [zh:吧] at the end softens the question, suggesting the speaker thinks this is likely true and is seeking confirmation.

on_screen_text.focus_text: “丝绸之路”也跟汉朝有关系吧？
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: “Sīchóu zhī Lù” yě gēn Hàncháo yǒu guānxì ba?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: The "Silk Road" is also related to the Han dynasty, right?

highlight_words.explanation_en: An ancient network of trade routes that connected the East and West.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: Literally 'Silk Road': 丝绸 (sīchóu, silk) + 之 (zhī, a particle similar to 'of') + 路 (lù, road).

highlight_words.explanation_en: A noun referring to a connection or association between people or things.

segments.visual_notes: Show an icon for the Silk Road (a camel on a path) and an icon for the Han Dynasty, with a question mark connecting them.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn to use '进行' for formal actions and '是从...开始的' for origins.

narration.subtitle_en: The guide confirms with [zh:对], 'Correct.' The main point is that trade with the West, [zh:跟西方进行贸易], started during the Han dynasty, [zh:是从汉朝开始的]. Notice the verb [zh:进行], which means 'to carry out' or 'conduct'. It's often used with formal, two-syllable verbs like [zh:贸易], trade. The structure [zh:是从...开始的] clearly marks the starting point of an activity.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 对，跟西方进行贸易是从汉朝开始的。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Duì, gēn xīfāng jìnxíng màoyì shì cóng Hàncháo kāishǐ de.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Correct. Trade with the West began in the Han dynasty.
on_screen_text.notes: 进行 (jìnxíng) is a formal verb often used with activities like 贸易 (màoyì, trade).

highlight_words.explanation_en: Literally 'west direction,' it refers to the Western world.
A formal verb used before a two-syllable action verb to indicate an extended or important activity.
The action of buying and selling goods and services.
A verb indicating the beginning of an action or event.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a map showing a trade route from Han China to the West.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn to talk about famous cultural products like Tang poetry.

narration.subtitle_en: The group moves on to the next great dynasty. The guide announces, [zh:我们来到唐朝了], 'We've come to the Tang dynasty.' A student then shares a personal connection, saying his dad often tells him that Chinese [zh:唐诗], Tang poetry, is the most famous, [zh:最有名]. The Tang dynasty is considered the golden age of Chinese poetry.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你们看，我们来到唐朝了。我爸爸常跟我说中国唐诗最有名。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐmen kàn, wǒmen láidào Tángcháo le. Wǒ bàba cháng gēn wǒ shuō Zhōngguó Tángshī zuì yǒumíng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Look, we've come to the Tang dynasty. My dad often tells me that Chinese Tang poetry is the most famous.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A powerful and prosperous dynasty (618–907 AD), known for its cultural achievements.
Poetry written during the Tang dynasty, considered a high point in Chinese literature.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: Composed of 唐 (Táng, from Tang dynasty) and 诗 (shī, poetry).

highlight_words.explanation_en: Literally 'to have a name,' this adjective means famous.

segments.visual_notes: Show an image of a classical Chinese scroll with poetry on it.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn vocabulary for poets and how to ask about past experiences with '过'.

narration.subtitle_en: The guide agrees with [zh:没错], 'That's right.' She adds that during the Tang dynasty, many great poets, [zh:大诗人], appeared, [zh:出现]. Then she asks a question to the student: [zh:你学过谁的诗], 'Whose poems have you studied?' The particle [zh:过] is very important here; it's used after a verb to indicate a past experience, something you have done before.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 没错，唐朝出现了很多大诗人。你学过谁的诗？
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Méicuò, Tángcháo chūxiànle hěn duō dà shīrén. Nǐ xuéguo shéi de shī?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: That's right, many great poets appeared in the Tang dynasty. Whose poems have you studied?

highlight_words.explanation_en: Literally 'not wrong,' this is a common expression for agreement.
A verb indicating that something or someone has come into sight or existence.
A person who writes poems.
highlight_words.character_insight_en: Composed of 诗 (shī, poetry) and 人 (rén, person).

segments.visual_notes: Animate the character '过' being added to the verb '学' to show the past experience meaning.

segments.teaching_goal: Learn how to answer a question about past experiences, naming a specific person.

narration.subtitle_en: This is a simple and direct answer to the previous question. The student replies, [zh:我学过李白的诗], 'I've studied Li Bai's poems.' This sentence follows the same 'Subject + Verb + 过 + Object' structure. He names [zh:李白], Li Bai, who is one of the most acclaimed poets from the Tang dynasty, often called the 'Poet Immortal'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我学过李白的诗。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ xuéguo Lǐ Bái de shī.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I've studied Li Bai's poems.
on_screen_text.notes: Li Bai (李白) is one of the most famous poets in Chinese history.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A famous Chinese poet from the Tang dynasty (701-762).

segments.visual_notes: Show a portrait of Li Bai next to the text.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand a sentence describing a famous person using the '...之一' structure.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence introduces a famous historical figure. It says: 'Li Bai is one of the most famous poets of the Tang dynasty.' Let's break it down. [zh:李白] is the name of the poet. [zh:唐朝] is the Tang dynasty. [zh:有名] means 'famous,' and [zh:诗人] means 'poet.' The key structure here is [zh:之一], which means 'one of.' It's used after a noun phrase to indicate that something is one member of a larger group.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 李白是唐朝最有名的诗人之一。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Lǐ Bái shì Tángcháo zuì yǒumíng de shīrén zhī yī.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Li Bai is one of the most famous poets of the Tang dynasty.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A person who writes poems. '诗' means poem and '人' means person.
A structure used to express 'one of...' a larger group. It's a bit more formal than using '的一个'.

segments.visual_notes: Show a portrait of Li Bai and an icon representing poetry (like a scroll and brush). Highlight '...之一' as a pattern.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand a cause-and-effect sentence explaining why many international students came to China.

narration.subtitle_en: The sentence continues, explaining the context. It starts with [zh:还有], meaning 'also' or 'in addition.' Then it uses a cause-and-effect pattern: 'because the Tang dynasty's economy and culture were very developed, many international students came to study.' The word [zh:那时] means 'at that time.' The cause is that the [zh:经济] (economy) and [zh:文化] (culture) were very [zh:发达] (developed). The result, introduced by [zh:所以], is that many [zh:外国留学生] (foreign international students) came to [zh:留学] (study abroad).

on_screen_text.focus_text: 还有，那时因为唐朝经济、文化非常发达，所以很多外国留学生来留学。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Háiyǒu, nàshí yīnwèi Tángcháo jīngjì, wénhuà fēicháng fādá, suǒyǐ hěn duō wàiguó liúxuéshēng lái liúxué.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Also, because the Tang dynasty's economy and culture were very developed, many international students came to study.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A shorthand for '那个时候', meaning 'that time' or 'then'.
Refers to the economic system of a country or area.
The customs, arts, and social institutions of a particular nation, people, or group.
Used to describe something that is highly developed, like an economy or technology.
A student who studies in a foreign country. '留学' means to study abroad, and '生' means student.

segments.visual_notes: Use icons for 'economy' (coins) and 'culture' (a book/mask). Animate an arrow from '发达' to '留学生' to show the connection.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand a short sentence of agreement and comparison.

narration.subtitle_en: This is a short, relatable response. The speaker says [zh:跟我们一样], which means 'Just like us.' This connects the experience of ancient international students to their own. The pattern [zh:跟]...[zh:一样] is very common for saying something is 'the same as' something else. Here, 'us' is the point of comparison.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 跟我们一样。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Gēn wǒmen yīyàng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Just like us.
on_screen_text.notes: Pattern: 跟 (with) + Noun + 一样 (the same)

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used to indicate similarity. Often paired with '跟' to form the structure '跟...一样' (the same as...).

segments.visual_notes: Show two groups of students (one ancient, one modern) side-by-side with an '=' sign between them.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to talk about past states using '曾经' and new vocabulary related to technology.

narration.subtitle_en: Here, the guide introduces a new topic. The sentence begins with [zh:你们知道吗], 'Did you know?' It then states, 'in history, China's science and technology were once very advanced.' [zh:在历史上] means 'in history.' The key subject is [zh:科学技术], science and technology. The word [zh:曾经] means 'once' or 'in the past,' indicating a past situation that may not be true now. Finally, [zh:先进的] means 'advanced.'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你们知道吗，在历史上，中国科学技术曾经是很先进的。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐmen zhīdào ma, zài lìshǐ shàng, Zhōngguó kēxué jìshù céngjīng shì hěn xiānjìn de.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Did you know that in history, China's science and technology were once very advanced?

highlight_words.explanation_en: A compound word. '科学' means science, and '技术' means technology or skill.
An adverb used to talk about actions or states that happened in the past.
Literally 'first to enter,' it means advanced or ahead of the times.

segments.visual_notes: Animate icons for science (atom) and technology (gears). Highlight the word '曾经' on a timeline graphic.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to ask for clarification using '你是说...吗？' and introduce a key cultural concept.

narration.subtitle_en: This is a clarifying question. The speaker asks, 'Are you talking about the Four Great Inventions?' The phrase [zh:你是说]...[zh:吗] is a useful way to confirm if you've understood someone correctly. It literally means 'Are you saying...?' The term [zh:四大发明] is a very important cultural concept, referring to the Four Great Inventions of ancient China.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你是说四大发明吗？
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐ shì shuō Sì Dà Fāmíng ma?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Are you talking about the Four Great Inventions?

highlight_words.explanation_en: A specific term for four inventions from ancient China celebrated for their historical significance. '四' is four, '大' is great, and '发明' is invention.

segments.visual_notes: Show a large question mark next to four mystery boxes, teasing the upcoming reveal.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the names of the Four Great Inventions.

narration.subtitle_en: The student confirms and lists the inventions. The line starts with [zh:我知道], 'I know.' Then it lists the four items: [zh:造纸] (papermaking), [zh:火药] (gunpowder), [zh:指南针] (the compass), and [zh:活字印刷] (movable-type printing). This is a straightforward list answering the previous implicit question.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我知道，是造纸、火药、指南针、活字印刷。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ zhīdào, shì zàozhǐ, huǒyào, zhǐnánzhēn, huózì yìnshuā.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I know, they are papermaking, gunpowder, the compass, and movable-type printing.

highlight_words.explanation_en: '造' means to make, and '纸' means paper.
'火' means fire, and '药' means medicine or chemical, so 'fire chemical'.
'指' means to point, '南' means south, and '针' means needle. A 'south-pointing needle'.
'活字' means movable type, and '印刷' means printing.

segments.visual_notes: As each invention is named, reveal an icon for it (a sheet of paper, a firecracker, a compass, a printing block).

segments.teaching_goal: To understand phrases describing when something was invented.

narration.subtitle_en: The guide confirms and adds more detail. Starting with [zh:对] (Correct), the sentence says, 'Papermaking and gunpowder were invented relatively early.' The verb here is [zh:发明], 'to invent.' The phrase [zh:较早] means 'relatively early,' providing a general timeframe without a specific date. It's a useful way to make comparisons in time.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 对，造纸、火药发明得较早。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Duì, zàozhǐ, huǒyào fāmíng de jiào zǎo.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Correct. Papermaking and gunpowder were invented relatively early.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Can be used as a verb (to invent) or a noun (invention).
'较' is short for '比较' (relatively), and '早' means early.

segments.visual_notes: Show a timeline. Place icons for papermaking and gunpowder on the 'early' side.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to describe development and reaching a high level.

narration.subtitle_en: This part gives more specific history. It says, 'Movable-type printing was invented in the Song dynasty, and the compass also reached a high level of development during the Song dynasty.' Notice the passive-like structure '[zh:是]...[zh:发明的]'. The verb [zh:发展] means 'to develop,' and [zh:水平] means 'level.' So, [zh:发展到了很高的水平] means 'developed to a very high level.'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 活字印刷是宋朝发明的，指南针也在宋朝发展到了很高的水平。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Huózì yìnshuā shì Sòngcháo fāmíng de, zhǐnánzhēn yě zài Sòngcháo fāzhǎn dàole hěn gāo de shuǐpíng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Movable-type printing was invented in the Song dynasty, and the compass also reached a high level of development during the Song dynasty.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Refers to growth or progress over time.
Literally 'water level,' it's used to describe a standard or level of achievement.

segments.visual_notes: On the timeline, place icons for movable-type printing and the compass under the 'Song Dynasty' section.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to identify the final item in a sequence using '最后一个'.

narration.subtitle_en: The tour moves forward in time. The guide says, 'We have now come to China's last dynasty: the Qing dynasty.' The key phrase is [zh:最后一个], which means 'the last one.' [zh:朝代] means 'dynasty.' The colon introduces the name of that last dynasty, which is the [zh:清朝], or Qing dynasty.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我们现在来到中国最后一个朝代：清朝。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒmen xiànzài láidào Zhōngguó zuìhòu yī gè cháodài: Qīngcháo.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: We have now come to China's last dynasty: the Qing dynasty.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Used to denote the end of a sequence or time period.
A period of rule by a line of rulers from the same family.

segments.visual_notes: Show a timeline of dynasties ending with the Qing Dynasty, which is highlighted.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand and use the question word '为什么'.

narration.subtitle_en: This is a simple but important question: 'Why is it the last one?' The question word [zh:为什么] means 'why.' It's formed by [zh:为] (for) and [zh:什么] (what), so it literally means 'for what reason?' This question prompts the explanation that follows.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 为什么是最后一个？
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wèishéme shì zuìhòu yī gè?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Why is it the last one?

highlight_words.explanation_en: The standard question word for asking for a reason.

segments.visual_notes: Display a large '为什么?' on screen with a question mark icon.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand a sentence explaining a major historical turning point.

narration.subtitle_en: This sentence answers the 'why' question. It says, 'Because in 1911, Sun Yat-sen led a revolution, and in 1912, the Republic of China was established. After that, there were no more emperors.' Key vocabulary includes [zh:领导] (to lead), [zh:革命] (revolution), and [zh:建立] (to establish). The final clause, [zh:就再也没有皇帝了], explains the ultimate consequence: 'then there were no more emperors.' The word [zh:皇帝] means 'emperor.'

on_screen_text.focus_text: 因为一九一一年孙中山领导革命，一九一二年建立了中华民国，就再也没有皇帝了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Yīnwèi yījiǔyīyī nián Sūn Zhōngshān lǐngdǎo gémìng, yījiǔyī'èr nián jiànlìle Zhōnghuá Mínguó, jiù zài yě méiyǒu huángdì le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Because in 1911, Sun Yat-sen led a revolution, and in 1912, the Republic of China was established. After that, there were no more emperors.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Can be a verb meaning 'to lead' or a noun for 'leader'.
Refers to a fundamental and relatively sudden change in political power. Here, it refers to the Xinhai Revolution of 1911.
Used for founding a country, organization, or system.
The supreme ruler of an empire.

segments.visual_notes: Show a portrait of Sun Yat-sen. Animate a crown icon for '皇帝' that then gets crossed out.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand a figurative expression summarizing the experience.

narration.subtitle_en: This final line reflects on their fast-paced tour. It says, 'We're really something, we've walked through several thousand years in just a couple of hours.' The phrase [zh:真不简单] means 'truly remarkable' or 'not simple.' It's a compliment. The second part, [zh:走过了几千年], 'walked through several thousand years,' is used figuratively to describe learning about a long period of history in a short time.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我们真不简单，一两个小时就走过了几千年。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒmen zhēn bù jiǎndān, yī liǎng gè xiǎoshí jiù zǒuguòle jǐ qiān nián.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: We're really something, we've walked through several thousand years in just a couple of hours.

highlight_words.explanation_en: Literally 'not simple,' this expression is used to praise someone or something as being impressive or extraordinary.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a clock with its hands spinning very fast over a long, scrolling timeline of Chinese history.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand how to express a perception with uncertainty using '好像'.

narration.subtitle_en: The first character says, [zh:我好像听到一个声音]. This means 'I think I hear a sound.' Let's break it down. [zh:我] is 'I'. [zh:好像] means 'to seem' or 'as if', expressing a feeling of uncertainty. [zh:听到] means 'to hear'. And [zh:一个声音] means 'a sound'. So, the speaker isn't completely sure, but they think they've heard something.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我好像听到一个声音。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ hǎoxiàng tīngdào yí ge shēngyīn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I think I hear a sound.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An adverb used to indicate that something appears to be the case, but the speaker is not entirely certain.
A noun referring to any kind of sound or a person's voice.

segments.visual_notes: Animate an ear icon with sound waves. Highlight '好像' to emphasize the uncertainty.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn the basic question pattern '什么 + Noun'.

narration.subtitle_en: In response, another character asks, [zh:什么声音]. This is a very simple and direct question meaning 'What sound?'. [zh:什么] is the question word for 'what', and [zh:声音] is the noun 'sound'. You can use this 'shénme + noun' pattern to ask about many things, like [zh:什么名字] for 'what name' or [zh:什么地方] for 'what place'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 什么声音？
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Shénme shēngyīn?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: What sound?

highlight_words.explanation_en: A question pronoun used to ask about the identity or nature of something.

segments.visual_notes: Show a large question mark next to the text. Animate the word '什么' to pop out.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand a metaphorical expression related to history.

narration.subtitle_en: The first speaker replies with a very poetic phrase: [zh:历史的脚步声]. This translates to 'The footsteps of history.' Let's look at the words. [zh:历史] is 'history'. The particle [zh:的] shows possession, like 's in English. And [zh:脚步声] means 'the sound of footsteps'. This isn't a literal sound, but a metaphor for feeling the presence and progression of history, especially fitting since they are in a museum.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 历史的脚步声。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Lìshǐ de jiǎobùshēng.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: The footsteps of history.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A noun referring to the study of past events.
A compound noun made of '脚步' (footstep) and '声' (sound).

segments.visual_notes: Show an animation of footprints appearing on an ancient scroll or map. Highlight the full phrase.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to use '也' to mean 'also' or 'too'.

narration.subtitle_en: Then, a third character chimes in with [zh:我也听到了一个声音]. This means 'I heard a sound too.' This sentence structure is very similar to the first line. The key new word here is [zh:也], which means 'also' or 'too'. It's placed right before the verb, [zh:听到], to show that this person shares the same experience as the first speaker.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 我也听到了一个声音。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Wǒ yě tīngdào le yí ge shēngyīn.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: I heard a sound too.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An adverb used to indicate that a situation is similar to one already mentioned.

segments.teaching_goal: To understand a colloquial expression for being hungry and how to anticipate what someone will say.

narration.subtitle_en: The first speaker interrupts with a guess: [zh:你要说肚子叫的]. This is a humorous line that means 'You mean the sound of a stomach growling...' Let's break it down. [zh:你要说] means 'you are going to say' or 'you mean'. The phrase [zh:肚子叫] is a very common way to say your stomach is growling. The ellipsis at the end shows the speaker is trailing off, waiting for confirmation.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你要说肚子叫的……
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐ yào shuō dùzi jiào de...
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: You mean the sound of a stomach growling...

highlight_words.explanation_en: A noun for the stomach or abdomen.

highlight_words.translation: to call; to make a sound
highlight_words.explanation_en: A verb that means to shout, call, or make a sound. In this context, it describes the noise a stomach makes.

segments.visual_notes: Show a cartoon stomach with sound waves coming out of it. Animate the ellipsis '...' to pulse.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to confirm something with '对了'.

narration.subtitle_en: The response is a simple and direct confirmation: [zh:对了]. This means 'Exactly' or 'That's right.' It's used to affirm that what the previous speaker guessed is correct. This is a very useful phrase for agreeing with someone in daily conversation.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 对了。
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Duì le.
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Exactly.

highlight_words.explanation_en: An expression used to confirm that something is correct or to suddenly remember something.

segments.visual_notes: Animate a checkmark icon next to the text to signify correctness.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to ask what people are talking about and the function of the particle '呀'.

narration.subtitle_en: Finally, a new person joins and asks, [zh:你们在说什么呀]. This means 'What are you guys talking about?'. [zh:你们] is the plural 'you'. [zh:在说] means 'are talking about', with [zh:在] indicating an ongoing action. [zh:什么] is 'what'. The final particle [zh:呀] is important here. It doesn't change the meaning but softens the tone of the question, making it sound more casual and friendly.

on_screen_text.focus_text: 你们在说什么呀？
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Nǐmen zài shuō shénme ya?
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: What are you guys talking about?

highlight_words.explanation_en: A sentence-final particle, often used in questions or exclamations to soften the tone and add a sense of friendliness or casualness.

segments.visual_notes: Show icons of the other characters with speech bubbles, and a new character entering with a question mark over their head.

segments.teaching_goal: To master the use of '之一' to indicate that something is one member of a larger group.

narration.subtitle_en: Now let's focus on a useful grammar structure: [zh:之一]. This is a more formal or literary way to say 'one of'. The character [zh:之] is a classical particle that often functions like the modern particle [zh:的]. So, [zh:之一] literally means 'of them, one'. We saw this in the opening narration: [zh:中国是世界文明古国之一], meaning 'China is one of the world's ancient civilizations.' Notice how it follows a noun phrase, [zh:世界文明古国], that defines the group. We also saw it used to describe Li Bai: [zh:李白是唐朝最有名的诗人之一], 'Li Bai is one of the most famous poets of the Tang dynasty.' It's a very common and elegant way to express this idea in written Chinese or formal speech.

on_screen_text.focus_text: Noun Phrase + 之一
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Noun Phrase + zhī yī
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: one of...
on_screen_text.notes: Used after a noun phrase to indicate 'one of' a larger group.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A particle combination used to express 'one of' a larger group, often in formal or written contexts.

grammar_points.explanation_en: China is one of the world's ancient civilizations.

grammar_points.explanation_en: Li Bai is one of the most famous poets of the Tang dynasty.

grammar_points.pattern: 造纸是中国的四大发明之一。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Zàozhǐ shì Zhōngguó de sì dà fāmíng zhī yī.
grammar_points.explanation_en: Papermaking is one of the four great inventions of China.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to use '其中' to refer back to a previously mentioned group.

narration.subtitle_en: Next, let's look at the word [zh:其中]. It means 'among them' or 'within it'. Let's break it down: [zh:其] is a pronoun, similar to 'that' or 'those', and [zh:中] means 'in' or 'among'. So, [zh:其中] literally means 'in that'. It's used to refer back to a group or whole that was just mentioned. In the dialogue, Li Wen says the National Museum is very large, and [zh:中国历史只是其中的一部分], 'the exhibit of Chinese history is only one part of it.' Here, [zh:其中] refers back to the 'National Museum of China'. It's crucial that the context provides a clear antecedent. For example: [zh:上个学期我选了三门课，其中金融课最难], 'Last semester I took three courses. Among them, Finance was the hardest.' Here, [zh:其中] refers to the 'three courses'.

on_screen_text.focus_text: Context (Group)..., 其中 + Comment
on_screen_text.focus_pinyin: Context (Group)..., qízhōng + Comment
on_screen_text.focus_gloss_en: Context (Group)..., among which/whom...
on_screen_text.notes: 其中 refers back to the previously mentioned group or whole.

highlight_words.explanation_en: A pronoun used to mean 'among them' or 'within it', referring back to a previously mentioned group.

grammar_points.pattern: 中国历史只是其中的一部分。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Zhōngguó lìshǐ zhǐ shì qízhōng de yí bùfen.
grammar_points.explanation_en: The exhibit of Chinese history is only one part of it.

grammar_points.pattern: 我选了三门课，其中金融课最难。
grammar_points.pattern_pinyin: Wǒ xuǎn le sān mén kè, qízhōng jīnróng kè zuì nán.
grammar_points.explanation_en: I took three courses. Among them, Finance was the hardest.

segments.segment_title: Setting the Scene: Lisa's History Course
segments.teaching_goal: To understand the sentence structure '除了...以外还...' (besides... also...) and learn vocabulary related to school courses.

narration_track.subtitle_en: This first part of the sentence sets the scene. It says that this semester, besides her Chinese class, Lisa also chose a Chinese history course. The key structure here is [zh:除了...以外，还...], which means 'besides... also...'. We see she [zh:选了], or 'chose', a course. The word for course is [zh:课], and the measure word for courses is [zh:门].

content.focus_text: 丽莎这个学期除了中文课以外还选了一门中国历史课。
content.focus_pinyin: Lìshā zhège xuéqī chúle Zhōngwén kè yǐwài hái xuǎn le yī mén Zhōngguó lìshǐ kè.
content.focus_gloss_en: This semester, besides Chinese class, Lisa also chose a Chinese history course.

segments.teaching_goal: To learn how to describe China as an ancient civilization and understand the phrase '...之一' (one of...).

narration_track.subtitle_en: Next, we get some background on China. It's described as one of the world's ancient civilizations. The phrase is [zh:世界文明古国之一]. Let's break it down: [zh:世界] is 'world', [zh:文明] is 'civilization', and [zh:古国] is 'ancient country'. The key grammar point here is [zh:之一], meaning 'one of'. The sentence continues that China has a written history of over four thousand years. [zh:有文字记载的历史] means 'a history with written records'.

content.focus_text: 中国是世界文明古国之一，有文字记载的历史就有四千多年。
content.focus_pinyin: Zhōngguó shì shìjiè wénmíng gǔguó zhī yī, yǒu wénzì jìzǎi de lìshǐ jiù yǒu sìqiān duō nián.
content.focus_gloss_en: China is one of the world's ancient civilizations, with a written history of over four thousand years.

A structure used to indicate that something is one member of a larger group.
